name: Amala Yoga — Benefic in 10th from Moon (Spotless Fame)
description: A natural benefic (Jupiter, Venus, Mercury) in the 10th house from Moon forms Amala Yoga, giving spotless, untarnished fame and pure public reputation.

source_text.quote: If a benefic planet is in the 10th from the Moon or from the Lagna, Amala Yoga is formed. The native will achieve lasting fame, will be charitable, and of spotless character.

all_of: A natural benefic (Jupiter, Venus, or Mercury) is in the 10th house from Moon

interpretation.meaning: Amala = spotless/pure. This yoga gives fame that is untarnished by scandal. The native is known for virtue, charity, and pure intentions. Structural lifetime promise.

metadata.notes: One of the most desirable fame yogas — fame without controversy. Activated during benefic dasha/transit periods.

name: 10th-1st Lord Exchange — Career-Self Fame Fusion
description: When the 10th lord and 1st lord exchange houses (mutual reception/Parivartana), the native's identity and public career merge, creating structural fame potential.

source_text.quote: The exchange of the lords of the 1st and 10th houses makes the native famous, powerful, equal to a king in his domain.

interpretation.meaning: The self (1st) and karma/public action (10th) exchange creates an inseparable link between identity and career. Whatever the native does professionally becomes their identity and vice versa.

metadata.notes: Strongest structural yoga for career-based fame. Activated when either lord runs in dasha.

name: Moon in 10th with 10th Lord in Trine — Public Popularity
description: Moon in the 10th house gives public visibility and emotional connection with masses. When the 10th lord is simultaneously in a trine (1/5/9), fame through popularity manifests.

source_text.quote: The Moon in the 10th house makes the native famous, successful in undertakings, and connected to the public. If the 10th lord is in a trine, this fame is dharmic and lasting.

all_of: Moon is in the 10th house from Lagna
10th lord is in a Trikona (1st, 5th, or 9th) from Lagna

interpretation.meaning: Moon in 10th gives innate public appeal — the masses emotionally connect with the native. Combined with 10th lord in trine, this becomes dharmic fame (righteous public standing).

metadata.notes: Politicians, public figures, and entertainers often have this combination.

name: Multiple Lords in 9th House — Dharma-Based Fame
description: When multiple house lords (especially 1st, 5th, 9th, 10th) are placed in the 9th house of dharma, the native gains fame through righteous action, teaching, or spiritual authority.

source_text.quote: When lords of auspicious houses congregate in the 9th, the native becomes a dharma-raja, famous for righteous conduct, blessed by fortune.

all_of: At least 2 lords from (1st, 5th, 9th, 10th, 11th) are placed in the 9th house

interpretation.meaning: The 9th house concentration of auspicious lords creates a dharmic fame pattern — known for wisdom, teaching, spiritual authority, or righteous leadership.

metadata.notes: Gurus, philosophers, religious leaders, and righteous kings have this pattern.

name: Vargottama Moon — International Recognition Potential
description: Moon in the same sign in both Rasi and Navamsa (Vargottama) gives exceptional emotional strength and international appeal. The native's public image transcends borders.

source_text.quote: A Vargottama Moon makes the native famous across lands, emotionally powerful, and recognized far from birthplace.

all_of: Moon is Vargottama (same sign in Rasi and Navamsa charts)

interpretation.meaning: Vargottama Moon = doubled emotional power. The native's emotional expression and public image carry across cultural boundaries, giving international fame potential.

metadata.notes: Actors, musicians, and political figures with international appeal often have Vargottama Moon.

name: 10th Lord with Natural Benefics — Honored Reputation
description: When the 10th lord is conjoined with or aspected by natural benefics (Jupiter, Venus), the native's professional reputation is honored and respected without controversy.

source_text.quote: The 10th lord associated with benefics makes the native perform noble deeds and gain an honored, respected reputation in public life.

all_of: 10th lord is conjoined with or aspected by natural benefics (Jupiter, Venus, or Mercury)

interpretation.meaning: Benefic influence on the 10th lord ensures that professional actions lead to an honored reputation. The native is respected and recognized for noble deeds.

metadata.notes: A baseline structural indicator. Enhances all fame transits when present.

name: Sun-Rahu-Venus Charisma Pattern — Magnetic Public Appeal
description: Sun (fame) + Rahu (amplification/masses) + Venus (charm/beauty) in mutual aspect or conjunction creates extraordinary charismatic appeal and magnetic public presence.

source_text.quote: When Sun, Rahu, and Venus are mutually connected, the native possesses extraordinary charm that draws masses, though controversy may follow.

all_of: Sun, Rahu, and Venus are mutually connected (conjunction, mutual aspect, or same sign)

interpretation.meaning: This triple combination creates extreme charisma — Sun gives authority, Rahu amplifies to masses, Venus adds charm. The result is magnetic public appeal, though not always controversy-free.

metadata.notes: Film stars, cult leaders, and mass politicians often have this pattern. Can attract both adoration and controversy.

name: Vargottama Lagna & Moon — Doubled Personality Fame
description: When both Lagna and Moon are Vargottama (same sign in Rasi and Navamsa), the native's personality is exceptionally strong, creating a memorable and famous public persona.

source_text.quote: A Vargottama Lagna gives the native strength of body and fame. Combined with Vargottama Moon, the native is remembered across generations.

all_of: Lagna (Ascendant) is Vargottama (same sign in Rasi and Navamsa)

interpretation.meaning: Double Vargottama (Lagna + Moon) creates an unusually strong, coherent personality that leaves lasting impressions. The native becomes famous for who they are, not just what they do.

metadata.notes: Historical figures with lasting fame often have at least one Vargottama point. Both together is rare and powerful.

name: Sun in 10th House — Political/Government Fame
description: Sun in the 10th house is the classic indicator of fame through authority, government service, or political leadership. The native shines in public life.

source_text.quote: The Sun in the 10th makes the native extremely intelligent, rich, strong, and famous. He will have conveyances and will be happy.

all_of: Sun is placed in the 10th house from Lagna

interpretation.meaning: Sun in 10th is the raja yoga of public visibility. The native naturally commands authority and receives government/institutional recognition. Classic indicator of political fame.

metadata.notes: One of the strongest single-planet placements for fame. Kings, presidents, and CEOs frequently have Sun in 10th.

name: Malefics in 3/6/11 — Fame Through Competitive Victory
description: Natural malefics (Mars, Saturn, Rahu) in upachaya houses (3, 6, 11) grow stronger over time, giving fame through competitive victory, overcoming enemies, and sustained effort.

source_text.quote: Malefics in the 3rd, 6th, and 11th houses give victory over enemies, competitive success, and growing fame through sustained effort and courage.

all_of: At least 2 natural malefics (Mars, Saturn, Sun, Rahu, Ketu) are placed in houses 3, 6, or 11

interpretation.meaning: Malefics in upachaya houses (3/6/11) grow stronger with age. They give fame through competition, defeating opponents, and sustained aggressive effort. Sports stars, warriors, and competitive leaders.

metadata.notes: Fame grows with age for these natives. Late-blooming success pattern. Athletes, military heroes, business competitors.
